candidates.title: No houses lost in Fort Simpson, N.W.T., wildfire
candidates.description: Some outbuildings have been damaged confirm officials on Saturday morning, with a challenging day ahead as fire grows in the south and continues to burn in residential areas
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kashechewan evacuees in Niagara Falls, Ont., get apology from mayor over homelessness comments
candidates.description: Evacuees from Kashechewan First Nation who have been living in Niagara Falls, Ont., for the last six months marched in sweltering heat through the city's downtown Friday to demand an apology over recent comments made by the city's former chief administrative officer and by the local mayor.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heartbroken girlfriend of B.C. captain tells of close call days before deadly sinking
candidates.description: The heartbroken girlfriend of a young charter boat captain feared drowned in a deadly sinking in B.C. waters last Sunday says she was aboard the vessel two days earlier when a broken door swung open and water flooded in.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Pancakes, pipelines and independence: politics serving up new flavour at this year’s Calgary Stampede
candidates.description: Generations of Canada’s political class have summited at the “Greatest Outdoor Show on Earth” to shake hands and flip pancakes. This year’s festival arrives with a looming referendum on Alberta’s future within Canada and, as of Thursday night, a multibillion-dollar pipeline bid.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fans get home court advantage as first ever professional basketball game tips-off in Whitehorse
candidates.description: The roar of a professional basketball crowd are sounds the Yukon had only heard on TV, until today. Basketball fans are excited following courtside access as the Canadian Elite Basketball League brings Whitehorse its first ever professional basketball game.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada wants to build up to 10 new nuclear plants. Will our pension funds pay for them?
candidates.description: Canada's ambitious new nuclear strategy has called for private investment to help expand the industry. Given that nuclear power plants are notoriously expensive to build, the government will have to heavily de-risk the ventures to attract large investors, like pension funds.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: The test of time: Canada’s only watchmaking school still ticking after 80 years
candidates.description: The École national d’horlogerie was founded after WW II as a way to help veterans reintegrate into society. Today, it’s about training professional watchmakers for the global market.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gordie Howe bridge cost still $6.4B despite delays, federal authority says
candidates.description: The cost of the new Gordie Howe bridge connecting Windsor and Detroit is still $6.4 billion despite multiple delays, according to the federal authority that oversees the bridge.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 26 points as Canada routs Puerto Rico 110-84 in basketball World Cup qualifying
candidates.description: Two-time NBA MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led all scorers with 26 points as Canada led from whistle-to-whistle in a 110-84 win over Puerto Rico on Friday in a FIBA Americas World Cup qualifying game.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. nurses to form picket lines at Vancouver General Hospital next week
candidates.description: Nurses will be escalating job action on Tuesday at 5:30 a.m. PT, the B.C. Nurses' Union announced Friday evening. It says essential services will be maintained.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Angry note left at scene of double murder of sons insisted father was the victim
candidates.description: In the house where police found two dead brothers — both boys believed to have been murdered by their father Mohammed Al-Lami before he died by suicide — investigators found a note left on the desk of Al-Lami's office, CBC News has learned.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Saskatoon teen wants apology after school removed her keffiyeh-inspired scarf during grad ceremony
candidates.description: A video of the incident at Centennial Collegiate's June 23 graduation ceremony has been viewed 16.5 million times and the school says its principal has been the target of abuse.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: StubHub sold ‘ghost tickets’ for World Cup months before real ones were issued, CBC finds
candidates.description: StubHub claims it doesn’t allow the listing of “speculative” tickets on its platform. But CBC News found “ghost tickets” were available for the FIFA World Cup months before they were issued — and was able to list non-existent tickets for the 2028 Olympics.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Yukon clinics are embracing AI — too quickly, some say
candidates.description: AI tools have been in use in some Yukon clinics for months— and they’ve become a valuable resource for healthcare providers. But others argue AI has been embraced too quickly, without fully understanding potential impacts on patients.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Calgary Stampede parade kicks off 10 days of western fun
candidates.description: A pair of Winter Olympians traded their skis and skates for hats and boots Friday as the Calgary Stampede parade kicked off 10 days of western-themed festivities.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Elections Alberta rejects Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition
candidates.description: Elections Alberta has rejected musician Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition, saying it failed to meet the requirements for a citizen initiative petition.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: More than 2,000 Quebec households stranded without a lease after July 1
candidates.description: More than 2,000 Quebec households are still without a home following the province's July 1 moving day. Housing advocates say the increase compared to last year shows that building more apartments isn't enough to solve the affordability crisis.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Pope Leo draws hard line on breakaway traditionalists, but it could get messy
candidates.description: As the Vatican excommunicated four bishops of the ultra-traditionalist Society of Saint Pius X this week, notice was also served that the society’s priests and lay Catholics who formally adhere to it are also considered cut off from the Roman Catholic Church — a move that raises the spiritual stakes for thousands of followers worldwide.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: St. Petersburg oil terminal hit as Ukraine presses long-range drone campaign
candidates.description: A Ukrainian drone attack struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg on Saturday, Russian officials said, as Kyiv pressed on with the bombardment of Russia’s oil infrastructure.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Crowds mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as Iran begins days-long funeral ceremonies
candidates.description: Hundreds of thousands of mourners began a days-long funeral on Saturday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s late supreme leader, beating their chests in sorrow before the glass case containing his flag-draped coffin in Tehran and calling for revenge against Israel and the United States.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gaza has become frozen in 'humanitarian purgatory' — and it could get worse
candidates.description: Nine months after Hamas and Israel agreed to a hard-fought ceasefire in Gaza, the term has become a cruel irony for the two million Palestinians trapped inside the territory.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'Our families were so scared': Indian sea captain fears for other seafarers still stuck in Persian Gulf
candidates.description: An Indian sea captain who was stuck on an oil tanker in the Persian Gulf for more than two months as the U.S. and Israel-Iran war raged says thousands of mariners are still at risk and don’t get the recognition they deserve.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Interpol names suspect in Monaco bombing that reportedly wounded Russia-linked tycoon
candidates.description: Interpol on Friday identified a 39-year-old woman from Ukraine as the main suspect in a bombing in Monaco that is thought to have targeted a Ukrainian tycoon with links to Russia.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta’s pipeline pitch is heavy on public, light on private investment — so who benefits?
candidates.description: Energy experts and economists say Alberta’s public-private pitch may be the only way to get a pipelines done in today's context, but if there are benefits, they could go beyond just the oil and gas industry.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada Post stopping parcel delivery to some EU countries because of new duty
candidates.description: Canada Post says it is suspending service to some European Union countries over new customs rules for low-value shipments, saying it's not accepting parcels destined for a dozen countries until further notice.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta pitches southern route for West Coast pipeline, with a price tag of $35B or more
candidates.description: Alberta is formally proposing an oil pipeline to the southwest coast of British Columbia for tanker export to Asian markets, in a partnership with federally-owned Trans Mountain Corp. and Calgary-based Pembina Pipeline Corp.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hoekstra says no 'significant progress' in 14 months of trade negotiations, but U.S. to keep talking
candidates.description: The U.S. envoy to Canada says 14 months of trade talk between Canada and the Trump administration has resolved few issues. But Pete Hoekstra insists the Americans will keep talking in the hopes of striking an agreement on the future of CUSMA, as well as settling other issues.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
candidates.description: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
candidates.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback
candidates.description: Serena Williams pulls out of her planned Wimbledon doubles appearance with older sister Venus because of a knee injury.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants as he visits gateway island
candidates.description: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
candidates.description: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
candidates.description: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
candidates.description: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
candidates.description: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Eala wins 'for all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks'
candidates.description: Alexandra Eala dedicates her stunning win over defending Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek to "all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
candidates.description: The couple tied the knot in New York on Friday, after months of speculation about when, where and how it would happen.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: In pictures: Stars and fans celebrate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
candidates.description: Celebrities and sports stars were spotted as the A-list couple tied the knot in New York on Friday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter' - Europe's new climate in seven charts
candidates.description: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Funeral of Iran's former supreme leader 'intensely political moment'
candidates.description: Authorities expect up to 20 million people to attend ceremonies across Iran and Iraq over the coming days.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: BBC in Tehran as mourners gather for former supreme leader's funeral
candidates.description: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed in NYC - here's how the day unfolded
candidates.description: The couple tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony officiated by comedian Adam Sandler at Madison Square Garden.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'It's so hot' - Dangerous hot weather grips parts of the US
candidates.description: More than 165 million people were sweltering on Friday under record temperatures along the US East Coast and Midwest, according to the US National Weather Service.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Parents warned not to publicly share children’s images amid AI abuse risks
candidates.description: The NCA says there is a growing threat of children's images being used to create child abuse material.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
candidates.description: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: A new phase in the war in Ukraine
candidates.description: Ukraine says it can now hit military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Former ambassador Daniel Fried explains why he thinks Russia is starting to lose its strategic advantage.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pope Leo visits Lampedusa to spotlight missing migrants
candidates.description: Pope Leo XIV will spend July 4th in Lampedusa, Italy, one of Europe's busiest migrant landing points. He will pray with migrants and honor those who died trying to cross the Mediterranean to Europe.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran begins week of funeral celebrations for Khamenei
candidates.description: Foreign dignitaries are gathering in Iran for a week of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: China's military promotes 2 new generals after anti-corruption purge thins ranks
candidates.description: The shake-up is believed to be an effort to ensure the military's loyalty to the Communist Party and its leader, Xi Jinping.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
candidates.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran
candidates.description: Khamenei, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed in an airstrike on Feb. 28 at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
candidates.description: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
candidates.description: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: July 4th events threatened by heat wave. And, Russia strikes on Ukraine's capital
candidates.description: July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a trouble-making seal
candidates.description: The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
candidates.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Move over Chutes & Ladders: Schisto & Ladders has educational value plus worms
candidates.description: Spread by parasitic worms that can infect via a splash of water, schistosomiasis causes debilitating symptoms. It hits kids hard. A board game teaches how to prevent it.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: CDC emails reveal challenges facing its next director
candidates.description: Emails from CDC officials released by Sen. Bernie Sanders reveal agency leaders dealing with political pressures during the Trump era and highlight challenges for the next director.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
candidates.description: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
candidates.description: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
candidates.description: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 4 at 9:51PM CDT until July 4 at 10:15PM CDT by NWS Norman OK
candidates.description: SVROUN The National Weather Service in Norman has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Northeastern Comanche County in southwestern Oklahoma... Grady County in central Oklahoma... Southeastern Caddo County in southwestern Oklahoma... Northwestern Cleveland County in central Oklahoma... Northwestern McClain County in central Oklahoma... * Until 1015 PM CDT. * At 951 PM CDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from near Tuttle to near Chickasha to near Lake Ellsworth, moving east at 35 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Southwestern Oklahoma City, western Norman, Moore, Chickasha, Newcastle, Blanchard, Tuttle, Elgin, Minco, Apache, Rush Springs, Fletcher, Cyril, Dibble, Sterling, Cole, Alex, Verden, Cement, and Amber.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued July 4 at 10:48PM EDT until July 4 at 11:45PM EDT by NWS Upton NY
candidates.description: SMWOKX The National Weather Service in Upton NY has issued a * Special Marine Warning for... Sandy Hook NJ to Moriches Inlet NY out 20 to 60 nm... * Until 1145 PM EDT. * At 1046 PM EDT, a gust front well out ahead ahead of thunderstorms was located along a line extending from 9 nm S of the entrance to New York Harbor to near Shark River Reef to 18 nm E of Barnegat Light Reef to 16 nm E of Garden State North Reef, moving E at 50 kt. HAZARD...Wind gusts in excess of 50 knots. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Boats could suffer significant structural damage in high winds. Small craft could capsize in suddenly higher waves. * The gust front will remain over mainly open waters.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 4 at 8:48PM MDT by NWS Albuquerque NM
candidates.description: At 848 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near Elida, or 26 miles southwest of Portales, moving southeast at 15 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph and nickel size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor damage to outdoor objects is possible. Locations impacted include... Elida.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Dense Fog Advisory issued July 4 at 9:48PM CDT until July 5 at 9:00AM CDT by NWS Chicago IL
candidates.description: * WHAT...Dense fog will reduce visibility to one quarter mile or less. * WHERE...Winthrop Harbor to Calumet Harbor IL. * WHEN...Until 9 AM CDT Sunday. * IMPACTS...Low visibility will make navigation difficult.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 4 at 9:47PM CDT by NWS Jackson MS
candidates.description: At 947 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Raymond, moving east at 20 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Jackson, Clinton, Pearl, Byram, Flowood, Richland, Florence, Raymond, Terry, Whites, and Monterey.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 4 at 9:45PM CDT until July 4 at 10:30PM CDT by NWS Springfield MO
candidates.description: SVRSGF The National Weather Service in Springfield has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southeastern Barton County in southwestern Missouri... Western Dade County in southwestern Missouri... * Until 1030 PM CDT. * At 944 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Golden City, or 7 miles southeast of Lamar, moving east at 25 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Lamar, Lockwood, Golden City, Arcola, Kenoma, Neola, Cedarville, Meinert, and Sylvania.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 4 at 10:45PM EDT by NWS Wilmington OH
candidates.description: At 1044 PM EDT, a strong thunderstorm was located over Lawrenceburg, moving east at 15 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Cincinnati, Covington, Florence, Independence, Norwood, Forest Park, Erlanger, Newport, Sharonville, Blue Ash, Springdale, Reading, Harrison, North College Hill, Edgewood, Elsmere, Wyoming, Cheviot, Fort Mitchell, and Villa Hills. This includes the following Interstates... I-71 in Ohio between mile markers 0 and 5. I-74 in Indiana between mile markers 168 and 171. I-74 in Ohio between mile markers 0 and 19. I-75 in Kentucky between mile markers 175 and 191. I-75 in Ohio between mile markers 0 and 17. If outdoors, consider seeking shelter inside a sturdy building. To report hazardous weather conditions, go to our website at weather.gov/iln and submit your report via social media, when you can do so safely.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 4 at 9:45PM CDT until July 4 at 10:30PM CDT by NWS Norman OK
candidates.description: SVROUN The National Weather Service in Norman has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Seminole County in east central Oklahoma... Southern Lincoln County in central Oklahoma... Northeastern Pontotoc County in east central Oklahoma... Pottawatomie County in central Oklahoma... Hughes County in southeastern Oklahoma... * Until 1030 PM CDT. * At 944 PM CDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 6 miles south of Dustin to near Holdenville to 3 miles southwest of Dale, moving southeast at 45 mph. A wind gust of 60 mph was measured at the Seminole Airport. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Shawnee, Seminole, Tecumseh, Holdenville, McLoud, Wewoka, Prague, Pink, Konawa, Wetumka, Byng, Meeker, Maud, Allen, Earlsboro, Bowlegs, Dustin, Asher, Tribbey, and Francis.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 4 at 10:44PM EDT by NWS Charleston WV
candidates.description: At 1043 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Lubeck, or near Parkersburg, moving east at 15 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph and half inch hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is possible. Locations impacted include... Parkersburg, Belpre, Lubeck, Mineralwells, Washington, Davisville, and Blennerhassett. This includes the following highways... Interstate 77 in West Virginia between mile markers 165 and 178. Route 50 in West Virginia between mile markers 3 and 14.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch issued July 4 at 9:43PM CDT until July 5 at 12:00AM CDT by NWS Norman OK
candidates.description: SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WATCH 460 REMAINS VALID UNTIL MIDNIGHT CDT TONIGHT FOR THE FOLLOWING AREAS IN OKLAHOMA THIS WATCH INCLUDES 16 COUNTIES IN CENTRAL OKLAHOMA CANADIAN CLEVELAND GRADY LINCOLN MCCLAIN OKLAHOMA POTTAWATOMIE IN EAST CENTRAL OKLAHOMA PONTOTOC SEMINOLE IN SOUTHEAST OKLAHOMA COAL HUGHES IN SOUTHERN OKLAHOMA GARVIN MURRAY STEPHENS IN SOUTHWEST OKLAHOMA CADDO COMANCHE THIS INCLUDES THE CITIES OF ADA, ANADARKO, BLANCHARD, CHANDLER, CHICKASHA, COALGATE, CONCHO, DAVENPORT, DAVIS, DUNCAN, EL RENO, HINTON, HOLDENVILLE, LAWTON, LINDSAY, MEEKER, MOORE, MUSTANG, NEWCASTLE, NORMAN, OKLAHOMA CITY, PAULS VALLEY, PRAGUE, PURCELL, SEMINOLE, SHAWNEE, STROUD, SULPHUR, TUTTLE, WELLSTON, WETUMKA, WEWOKA, WYNNEWOOD, AND YUKON.
candidates.author: National Weather Service
